tagline: The relaunched hero, plated for WeHo: smoky-sweet chicken al pastor on cilantro-lime rice with fresh-tomato + corn salsa and a chipotle-honey vinaigrette drizzle.
ingredients: corn salsa (poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
signal_rank_note: Al pastor is not in the West Hollywood top-15 signal ranking; the local-evidence anchor here is the deliverable Chicken Al Pastor protein itself (7 competitive + 2 leading mentions in WeHo per get_evidence_counts) combined with Chipotle's own LTO equity. This is a pantry-anchored ship-now, not a top-of-signal play.
novelty_check: Chipotle's existing_dishes list returned empty in get_brand_existing_dishes. Chicken Al Pastor is an active/recurring protein per LTO history (returned Feb 10, 2026 per Chipotle newsroom), but this specific bowl configuration with the chipotle-honey vinaigrette finish is a configuration, not a duplicate listed menu item.
confidence_reason: compute_confidence returned 51/100 for Chipotle × al pastor × West Hollywood. Components: trend 14.3, local 11.7, maturity 85 (Established), feasibility 100 (deliverable), recency 66.7, lto 100. Held back by modest WeHo trend and local density; propped up by max feasibility and a strong LTO record (newsroom.chipotle.com 2023-03-14 and 2026-01-27).
comp_context: Mexican fine-casual in the WeHo trade area shows up in our scrape via Gracias Madre (verified via verify_restaurant_real). That gives a credible local reference point for smoke-and-citrus Mexican flavor profiles — useful as an aspirational ceiling, not a 1:1 comp.

dish_potential: Mole is the #1 ranked flavor in West Hollywood per get_signal_ranking (signal 0.881, DMA trend 76.3, 31 indie mentions across 23 reviews) and has 19 competitive + 4 leading mentions locally per get_evidence_counts. A shelf-stable mole sauce SKU (added as a salsa-station drizzle, not a protein) would let Chipotle ship a 'Mole-Drizzled' build over existing barbacoa or chicken, addressing the highest WeHo signal without disturbing line ops. The brand has no mole LTO in its history (per get_brand_lto_history), so this is genuine white space — not a relaunch.
operational_lift: Low per compute_lift_tier (1 missing SKU: mole, kind=sauce_shelf_stable, lift=low, portability=national). No fresh-cook or new-station requirements. Drizzle-format integration is the lowest-disruption way to introduce a new sauce in the Chipotle line.
comp_context: Verified WeHo Mexican comp in our scrape: Gracias Madre (verified via verify_restaurant_real). Gracias Madre's plant-forward Mexican positioning is a useful adjacent reference for how mole reads to a WeHo audience — premium, ceremonial, Oaxaca-coded — even though Chipotle would deliver it at a very different price point.

tagline: Black lentils and harissa-honey chicken on super greens with crazy feta, sumac slaw, and a hot harissa vinaigrette finish — a Mediterranean protein-and-pulse build for the WeHo lunch crowd.
ingredients: super greens (raw cabbage, chicory, brussels, broccoli, kale)
signal_rank_note: Lentils sits at rank #14 in the West Hollywood top-15 (signal 0.325, DMA trend 41.6, 7 indie mentions per get_evidence_counts — 3 competitive + 4 leading). Not a top-of-board signal but a real, deliverable Mediterranean anchor in a city where higher-ranked CAVA-compatible flavors (cauliflower, burrata, truffle) are not in the pantry. The build leans on harissa (deliverable, pantry-matched, pairing score 1.0 with lentils per get_pairing_score — same Mediterranean family) to add craveability.
novelty_check: get_brand_existing_dishes returned an empty existing-dishes list for CAVA. Black lentils, harissa honey chicken, crazy feta and sumac slaw are all current pantry SKUs per get_brand_pantry; this configuration as a named bowl is not in the returned existing dishes.
confidence_reason: compute_confidence returned 47/100 for CAVA × lentils × West Hollywood. Components: trend 41.6, local 6.7 (the soft spot — lentils is real in WeHo but not loud), maturity 65 (Steady), feasibility 100 (deliverable), recency 71.4, lto 0. Score is honest about the local-density gap; lift_tier is low and the dish leans on harissa-honey chicken to carry craveability.
comp_context: No verified WeHo Mediterranean comp surfaced in the restaurants I checked against verify_restaurant_real. The WeHo signal ranking itself (mole, miso, pesto, truffle dominating) tells us Mediterranean lentil dishes are an underserved adjacency rather than a crowded category here — which is the case for shipping it rather than a comp-driven one.

dish_potential: Cauliflower is the #5 West Hollywood signal (signal 0.691, DMA trend 38.2, 47 indie mentions, 43 reviews per get_signal_ranking) and the only top-5 WeHo flavor that is unambiguously on-brand for CAVA's Mediterranean vegetable-forward identity. get_evidence_counts shows it deeply established locally: 14 competitive + 24 leading mentions across 38+ restaurants, recent_12mo_mentions 19. compute_confidence returns 62/100 — a respectable peer for the Steady/Rising tier. The play: roast a whole-floret cauliflower SKU (separate from the existing brussels/carrot/cauliflower roasted-veg mix, which currently buries it) and let it land as a hero with harissa, lemon herb tahini, sumac slaw and crazy feta — fully on-pantry around the missing piece.
operational_lift: Medium per compute_lift_tier (1 missing SKU: cauliflower, kind=fresh_produce, lift=medium, portability=national). Fresh-produce additions carry more line burden than a sauce SKU, but CAVA already runs a roasted-vegetable station that includes a cauliflower component in the mix — the operational delta is spinning it off as its own portion rather than a wholly new prep stream.
comp_context: No verified WeHo cauliflower-anchored Med spot surfaced in verify_restaurant_real. Crossroads Kitchen (verified) is the closest in-scrape reference — a high-profile WeHo plant-forward restaurant where vegetable-as-hero plating is the operating premise. It supports the read that WeHo diners will pay top-of-category prices for a cauliflower that's treated as the centerpiece, not a side.

tagline: Apple kimchi and miso-glazed salmon over wild rice and shredded kale, finished with crispy rice, nori sesame, and a miso sesame ginger dressing — a Koreatown-adjacent build for WeHo.
signal_rank_note: Kimchi is rank #8 in the West Hollywood top-15 (signal 0.536, DMA trend 50.5, 17 indie mentions per get_evidence_counts — 16 competitive + 1 leading). The competitive-pool concentration (vs. just 1 leading mention) tells us kimchi is a working-class WeHo signal showing up in everyday Korean-adjacent spots more than at marquee venues — a good fit for a fast-casual price-point.
novelty_check: Checked against Sweetgreen's existing_dishes list. Closest neighbors are Miso Glazed Salmon (a plate, not a bowl, and kimchi-free), Crispy Rice Bowl (no kimchi, no salmon as listed), and Saucy KBBQ Chicken (KBBQ-anchored, chicken protein, different sauce family). This build is differentiated by the apple kimchi + miso salmon pairing on a wild-rice + kale base, which doesn't match any listed dish by name or ingredient profile. Kimchi + miso pairing scored 0.85 (allowed, korean/japanese cross-family) per get_pairing_score.
confidence_reason: compute_confidence returned 54/100 for Sweetgreen × kimchi × West Hollywood. Components: trend 50.5, local 26.7, maturity 85 (Established), feasibility 100 (deliverable from current pantry), recency 29.4 (the weakness — only 5 of 17 mentions in last 12mo, suggesting kimchi has plateaued in WeHo indie reviews even as Google Trends stays warm), lto 0. Lift is low per compute_lift_tier.
comp_context: No verified Koreatown-anchor WeHo restaurant surfaced via verify_restaurant_real in the names I checked (Genwa, Soban not in scrape). The local signal still reads as competitive-pool driven — 16 of 17 mentions are competitive, meaning kimchi shows up at everyday spots that Sweetgreen's lunch customer is realistically substituting against, which is exactly the comp surface you want for a ship-now build.

dish_potential: Truffle is the #4 West Hollywood signal (signal 0.722, DMA trend 44.3, 188 indie mentions across 158 reviews per get_signal_ranking) and produces the highest confidence score of any Sweetgreen gap-fill candidate I tested: 62/100 with a maximum local component (100), reflecting 74 competitive + 82 leading mentions across 38 restaurants and 85 recent-12mo mentions per get_evidence_counts. Maturity reads Peak — truffle is a saturated WeHo signifier and the question is whether Sweetgreen can monetize it at fast-casual price. The play: a truffle-oil-finished mushroom build over the existing warm portobello mix + shaved parmesan + parmesan crisps + garlic breadcrumbs SKUs, leaning on the existing Shroomami DNA but with a clear WeHo regional spin. Treated as a regional LTO (truffle is portability=regional per compute_lift_tier), not a national rollout.
operational_lift: High per compute_lift_tier (1 missing SKU: truffle, kind=specialty_premium, lift=high, portability=regional). Specialty-premium SKUs carry meaningful cost, spoilage and training overhead; portability is regional, not national, which is why the recommendation explicitly frames this as a West Hollywood/LA-market LTO rather than a system play. compute_confidence was 62/100 — the strongest gap-fill score in this brief — which justifies the lift specifically here.
comp_context: Verified WeHo truffle-heavy comps in our scrape: Cecconi's West Hollywood (verified) and The Henry (verified). Both are exactly the kind of higher-check-average venues where truffle is currently a core menu signifier in the neighborhood — they define the local taste reference Sweetgreen would be selling a fast-casual translation of. That positioning is opinion, not a verified brand-behavior claim.
